events.event_description: John Brinkerhoff Jackson presented credentials as the first U.S. Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Montenegro, establishing nonresident envoy-level relations. Ministers were based in Athens, not resident in Montenegro.

evidence.quote: John Brinkerhoff Jackson, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1905-03-08. Credentials presented: 1905-10-30. Presented recall on 1907-10-27.

events.event_description: Montenegro was absorbed into the Kingdom of the Serbs, Croats, and Slovenes, ending U.S. diplomatic representation to Montenegro.

evidence.quote: Montenegro was absorbed into the Kingdom of the Serbs, Croats, and Slovenes on 1918-12-04.

events.event_description: Consulate Podgorica was elevated to Embassy status, coinciding with the presentation of credentials by Chargé d'Affaires ad interim Arlene Lorraine Ferrill, following Montenegro's independence.

evidence.quote: Arlene Lorraine Ferrill, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 2006-10-05. Note: Consulate Podgorica was elevated to Embassy status on October 5, 2006.
